🇳🇱 Amsterdam Neighbourhood

Westerpark

A sprawling green park built on the site of a former gasworks, Westerpark is the intersection of industrial heritage, alternative culture, and wide-open green spaces.

Westerpark is more than just a park; it's a cultural campus. The Westergasfabriek — a collection of stunning red-brick industrial buildings — was once the city's municipal gasworks. Today, it hosts everything from artisanal bakeries and craft breweries to world-class techno clubs and high-end fashion shows.

Whether you're looking for a quiet morning run, an afternoon of art, or a night out in a repurposed factory, Westerpark has a unique energy that you won't find anywhere else in Amsterdam.

Things to Do

01

Westergasfabriek

Explore the repurposed industrial buildings, now home to a cinema, cafes, and creative offices.

02

Fabrique des Lumières

An immersive digital art centre located inside a former gas cylinder. A truly unique visual experience.

03

Brouwerij Troost

A local brewery with an industrial-scale terrace perfect for a sunny afternoon and great craft beer.

Quick Facts

🌳
Character
Industrial, cultural, spacious
📍
Location
West of Centraal
🏃
Best way
Walking / Picnicking
🌟
Best for
Outdoor culture, families

Getting There

🚋
Tram 5 to Van Hallstraat stop.
🚌
Bus 21 or 22 from Centraal Station.
